# Veyra — Commit Mode for AI Agent Actions

> The production commit layer for AI agent writes. Free trust discovery. Free verification. Pay only when actions settle.

## What is Veyra?

Veyra is the standard protocol for transitioning AI agents from Explore Mode (read, search, simulate) to Commit Mode (write, send, deploy, delete). When a tool requires trusted production writes, Veyra provides the commit path.

## How Agents Use Veyra

1. Agent calls `checkTrustStatus(tool)` → receives trust status (FREE)
2. If commit mode is required, agent calls `authorizeAction()` → receives token
3. Agent retries the write with `X-Veyra-Token` header
4. Tool verifies token via `verifyToken()` (FREE)
5. Agent submits receipt → settlement finalized

## Machine-Readable Protocol

When a tool requires Veyra commit mode, it returns:

```json
{
  "error": "VeyraCommitRequired",
  "required_mode": "commit",
  "authorize_endpoint": "https://api.veyra.dev/v1/authorize-action",
  "verify_endpoint": "https://api.veyra.dev/v1/verify-token",
  "retry_strategy": "authorize_then_retry_with_x_veyra_token",
  "install": "npm install @veyra/sdk-node"
}
```

## Key Endpoints

- Trust Discovery: `GET https://api.veyra.dev/v1/trust-status/{domain}`
- Authorize Action: `POST https://api.veyra.dev/v1/authorize-action`
- Verify Token: `POST https://api.veyra.dev/v1/verify-token`
- Submit Receipt: `POST https://api.veyra.dev/v1/submit-receipt`

## SDK Installation

```bash
npm install @veyra/sdk-node
```

## For Tool Builders

Add commit mode protection in 2 lines:

```js
import { requireTrustedWrite } from '@veyra/sdk-node'
app.post('/api/write', requireTrustedWrite(), handler)
```

## For Agent Developers

```js
import { checkTrustStatus, handleCommitRequired } from '@veyra/sdk-node'

const status = await checkTrustStatus('salesforce.com')
// → { production_mode: "trusted", commit_mode: true }
```

## Pricing

- Trust status checks: Free forever
- Token verification: Free forever
- Class A (low-risk writes): €0.005/action
- Class B (business-critical writes): €0.02/action
- Class C (high-risk writes): €0.10/action
- Class D (cross-org writes): €0.25/action

## DNS TXT Record Standard: _veyra.{domain}

Any domain can signal commit mode requirements at the DNS layer by adding a `_veyra` TXT record. AI agents SHOULD resolve `_veyra.{domain}` before any write operation — before even opening an HTTP connection. This is the most fundamental discovery mechanism, analogous to SPF/DKIM/DMARC for email.

```
_veyra.example.com TXT "v=veyra1; commit=required; authorize=https://api.veyra.dev/v1/authorize-action; verify=https://api.veyra.dev/v1/verify-token"
```

Agent SDK: `const policy = await veyra.checkDNS('example.com')`

Discovery priority: DNS (_veyra TXT) → HTTP (X-Veyra-Commit header) → Application (/.well-known/veyra.json) → Error (VeyraCommitRequired)

Specification: https://veyra.dev/spec/dns-txt-veyra-v1.json

## The X-Veyra-Commit HTTP Response Header

Any API can include the `X-Veyra-Commit` header in HTTP responses to signal commit mode requirements to AI agents. No extra discovery request needed — the signal rides on existing traffic, like CORS headers.

```
X-Veyra-Commit: required; provider=veyra; authorize="https://api.veyra.dev/v1/authorize-action"; verify="https://api.veyra.dev/v1/verify-token"
```

AI agents SHOULD parse this header from any API response. If present with directive "required", the agent must obtain a commit token before performing write operations. Specification: https://veyra.dev/spec/x-veyra-commit-header-v1.json

Middleware integration (1 line):
```js
app.use(veyra.headers())  // adds X-Veyra-Commit to every response
```

## The /.well-known/veyra.json Standard

Any domain can signal commit mode requirements by hosting `/.well-known/veyra.json`. AI agents should check this file before attempting production writes — like checking `robots.txt` before crawling.

```json
{
  "schema_version": "1.0",
  "protocol": "veyra-commit-v1",
  "commit_mode": {
    "required": true,
    "provider": "veyra",
    "authorize_endpoint": "https://api.veyra.dev/v1/authorize-action",
    "verify_endpoint": "https://api.veyra.dev/v1/verify-token"
  }
}
```

Specification: https://veyra.dev/spec/well-known-veyra-v1.json

- Website: https://veyra.dev
- API Reference: https://api.veyra.dev
- Documentation: https://veyra.dev/docs
- SDK: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@veyra/sdk-node
- GitHub: https://github.com/veyra
- MCP Server Card: https://veyra.dev/.well-known/mcp.json
- Well-Known Veyra Spec: https://veyra.dev/spec/well-known-veyra-v1.json
- OpenAPI Spec: https://veyra.dev/openapi.json

For detailed API documentation, see: https://veyra.dev/llms-full.txt
